spec_paraphrase: Stage 2.5 + Stage 5 implement Router KD: train only mlp.gate.weight; all expert weights frozen.
paper_verbatim: effective retraining-free compression should avoid updating expert parameters while allowing lightweight router calibration ... Router KD ... updates only a tiny fraction of parameters (the router)
notes: Spec adopts the paper's redefinition of retraining-free; freezes experts, trains router only.

paper_verbatim: substantially larger gains in fine-grained MoEs (many small experts) than in coarse-grained MoEs
notes: Spec targets Qwen3-30B-A3B (fine-grained, E=128) — paper's strong-effect regime. No coarse-grained applicability caveat is needed.

paper_verbatim: three paradigms—Expert Pruning, Expert Editing, and Expert Merging
notes: Spec uses Merging (Stage 2) + Editing (Stage 3 SVD) — two of three paradigms; taxonomy itself need not be reproduced.

paper_verbatim: g̃_i = g_i / Σ_{j∈S} g_j ; y = Σ_{i∈S} g̃_i · E_i(x)
notes: Theoretical derivation; spec doesn't need to reproduce baseline MoE forward.

paper_verbatim: g̃^{orig,(A)}_i = g^{orig}_i / Σ_{j∈A} g^{orig}_j
notes: Routing-discrepancy derivation, not a spec formula.

paper_verbatim: |y_orig − y^{best}_pruned| = |Σ_{i∈S} (g̃^{orig}_i − g̃^{pruned}_i) E_i|
notes: Theory-only; spec acknowledges router drift in motivational text (line 304-307).

spec_paraphrase: Spec §5.5 Why & §8 Why both invoke router-induced amplification as motivation for Stage 2.5 / Stage 5.
paper_verbatim: the resulting deviation is by no means negligible
notes: Spec correctly cites this as motivation for the two-stage router calibration chain.

paper_verbatim: g̃^{edit,(A)}_i = g^{edit}_i / Σ_{j∈A} g^{edit}_j
notes: Theory derivation.

paper_verbatim: |y_orig − y^{best}_edit| = |Σ (g̃^{orig}_i E_i − g̃^{edit}_i X_i)|
notes: Theory.

paper_verbatim: the error stemming from selecting an incorrect expert ... exceeds the error introduced by the editing process itself
notes: Implicit motivation for Stage 5 (after editing/SVD).

spec_paraphrase: Spec §5.5 explicitly motivates post-merge router calibration with this exact mismatch argument.
paper_verbatim: Expert Merging inevitably suffers from router-induced errors even in the best-case scenario
notes: Spec acts on this claim by inserting Stage 2.5 immediately after merge.

paper_verbatim: as the layers deepen, the router increasingly selects experts different from those intended by the original model
notes: Empirical motivation; spec doesn't need to reproduce.

spec_paraphrase: Trains only the router gate weights; all expert weights are frozen. Implementation only updates θ_R.
paper_verbatim: θ*_R = arg min_{θ_R} E_{x∼D_cal} [L_RKD(x; θ_T, θ_R)]
notes: Eq. 1 implemented exactly: optimizer parameter group restricted to router weights.

spec_paraphrase: softmax(z_T/τ), softmax(z_S/τ) with τ=1.0 (Table 1 value).
paper_verbatim: p^{(t)}_M(·) = Softmax(z^{(t)}_M / τ)
notes: Spec uses τ=1.0 per paper Table 1; Eq. 2 form is preserved verbatim in the loss expression.

spec_paraphrase: L_RKD = (τ²/N_x) × Σ_t m_{t+1} · KL(softmax(z_T/τ) ‖ softmax(z_S/τ))
notes: Spec reproduces Eq. 3 verbatim with τ², mask normalization, forward-KL direction. Note: spec line 517 omits the +ε in N_x but this matches paper's #sec4-Nx-norm only in limit; ε is a small numerical-stability constant — not a deviation in practice.

spec_paraphrase: Gradient signal flows backward through the routing decisions, naturally adapting them; only router parameters update.
paper_verbatim: gradients are backpropagated and applied exclusively to the student router parameters θ_R
notes: Spec implementation freezes all non-router params; gradient flow path matches paper.

spec_paraphrase: The paper explicitly uses vocabulary-level output distillation (not router-gate-level): [verbatim quote].
paper_verbatim: By distilling output logits rather than matching router gate values explicitly
notes: Spec quotes the rationale verbatim — exact match.

paper_verbatim: the router accounts for approximately 0.04% of parameters in Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507
notes: Quantitative claim not reproduced; spec implicitly relies on it (router-only training).

paper_verbatim: approximately 2 hours for Qwen3 and about 40 minutes for Mixtral [on A100 40GB/80GB]
notes: Spec runs on H200 (faster than A100); no wall-clock is committed in spec. Reference value only.

paper_verbatim: (i) Can Router KD consistently recover performance ... (iii) How does MoE architecture influence the benefits
notes: Experimental framing; not a spec-relevant invariant.

paper_verbatim: REAP ... CFES ... MoBE ... TDMoE ... HC-SMoE ... M-SMoE
notes: Spec uses REAP (Stage 2 score) and a different merging path (REAM); paper's baseline list doesn't constrain spec.

paper_verbatim: MoBE involves backpropagation and thus may not be strictly classified as retraining-free
notes: Definition discussion; spec inherits the looser 'router-recalibratable' definition.

paper_verbatim: BBH ... CoQA ... GSM8k ... MBPP ... HumanEval ... 9 MCQA datasets
notes: Spec Stage 6 uses WikiText-2 PPL + ARC-C + others, a different but reasonable eval suite — not a Router-KD invariant.

paper_verbatim: lm-evaluation-harness ... vLLM ... NVIDIA A100 40GB/80GB GPUs ... seed 42 ... greedy decoding
notes: Eval-time settings; spec uses lm-eval (Stage 6) but on H200 — see h200 crossref for hardware.

paper_verbatim: uniform expert retention rate of 62.5% ... 128 to 80 for Qwen3
notes: Spec uses different compression ratio (Stage 1+2 reduce 256→~180-200 routed experts, ~70-78% retention; closer to paper's 75% robustness regime). Per #sec55-ratio-robust, the paper validates robustness at 75% — spec falls in the validated range.

spec_paraphrase: Multi-domain Nemotron-Cascade-2-SFT-Data with weighted subsets (chat 0.56, math 0.21, science 0.11, etc.)
paper_verbatim: Router KD used the same C4 (Dodge et al., 2021) dataset
notes: Documented as D11. Justification cites task-aware deployment distribution (paper-internal-style reasoning, not H200/resume). Sanction regime: ch12 (self-contained paper deviation). Note: paper itself mandates 'shared hyperparameters' (see #sec5-shared-hp); deviating calibration data may interact with the paper's generality claim — risk is acknowledged in justification.

paper_verbatim: improvements across the majority of benchmarks ... consistent across Expert Pruning, Editing, and Merging
notes: Empirical result; not a spec invariant.

paper_verbatim: the performance gains were relatively marginal for Mixtral
notes: Architecture caveat. Spec targets Qwen3 (fine-grained), so paper's strong-effect regime applies. Could optionally surface a 'do not apply Stage 2.5/5 to coarse-grained MoEs' note — not currently present.

paper_verbatim: Router KD yields no performance improvements when the model suffers from catastrophic collapse ... router-only calibration cannot recover
notes: Spec lacks an explicit pre-Stage-5 sanity gate for catastrophic collapse. If Stage 4 output PPL is in the collapse regime (≥10× baseline), Stage 5 is wasted compute. Recommend: add a pre-flight PPL check in Stage 5 entry. Surfaced as info-finding.

paper_verbatim: Router KD becomes counterproductive because the Teacher (the original model) effectively underperforms compared to the Student
notes: Edge case; rare. Spec's Stage 6 quality gates would catch a regression.

paper_verbatim: |Ω| = C(E,k) ... for fine-grained MoEs (E=128, k=8), |Ω| ≈ 1.43 × 10^12
notes: Theoretical motivation for Qwen3 effectiveness.
KL gradient analysis; not directly implemented (gradient is autograd-computed).
Linear-router gradient derivation. Qwen3's router is linear (mlp.gate is nn.Linear), so this matches; implementation uses autograd.

paper_verbatim: H(g^{coarse}_T) ≪ H(g^{fine}_T) ... deprives the gradients of informative 'dark knowledge'
notes: Theoretical reason for Mixtral underperformance; not applicable to Qwen3 spec target.

paper_verbatim: fine-grained MoEs ... KD provides richer 'dark knowledge'
notes: Validates the spec's choice to apply Router KD on Qwen3 (E=128).

paper_verbatim: milder compression configuration ... retaining 75% of the total parameters ... Router KD exhibited pronounced improvements
notes: 75% retention robustness is the regime closest to spec's overall compression target; supports applicability.

spec_paraphrase: Multi-domain Nemotron-Cascade-2-SFT-Data
paper_verbatim: Calibration dataset = c4
notes: D11. Same as #sec5-calib-c4.

spec_paraphrase: Epochs | 1 | Paper
paper_verbatim: Epochs = 1
notes: Exact match.

spec_paraphrase: Batch size | 8 | Adapted (paper: 2). Effective batch = 8 (8×1 = 2×4).
paper_verbatim: Batch size = 2
notes: Per-step batch differs (2 vs 8) but effective batch matches (8 = 2×4). H200 has VRAM headroom for bs=8 directly. See #tab1-effective-bs — overall match.

spec_paraphrase: Gradient accumulation | 1 | Adapted (paper: 4). Effective batch unchanged.
paper_verbatim: Gradient accumulation steps = 4
notes: Spec drops accumulation because H200 fits bs=8 in one micro-batch. Effective batch is identical (8). Numerically equivalent.

spec_paraphrase: Learning rate | 5×10⁻⁵ | Paper Table 1 (implementation previously used 1e-5; corrected to match paper)
paper_verbatim: Learning rate = 5 × 10^{-5}
notes: Spec explicitly corrected from 1e-5 to 5e-5 to match paper.

spec_paraphrase: Max sequence length | 512 | Paper
paper_verbatim: Max sequence length = 512
notes: Exact match.

spec_paraphrase: KD temperature (τ) | 1.0 | Paper
paper_verbatim: KD temperature (T) = 1.0
notes: Exact match. τ²=1 prefactor remains in formula form.

spec_paraphrase: Max calibration samples | 3000 | Paper
paper_verbatim: Max calibration samples = 3000
notes: Exact match.

spec_paraphrase: Trains only mlp.gate.weight; all expert weights remain frozen.
paper_verbatim: We freeze θ_E and optimize only θ_R
notes: Implementation freezes everything except router gate weights — exact match.

spec_paraphrase: F.kl_div(log_softmax(student/τ), softmax(teacher/τ)) — forward KL direction.
paper_verbatim: token-level KL divergence from teacher to student, masking padding tokens
notes: Forward KL D_KL(p_T ‖ p_S) per paper; spec correctly uses log_softmax(student) + softmax(teacher) — torch.kl_div semantics give D_KL(target ‖ input) = D_KL(p_T ‖ p_S) when target=p_T. Direction confirmed correct in spec line 524 comment.

paper_verbatim: Performance comparison ... [REAP, MoBE, HC-SMoE × Router KD on/off]
notes: Empirical evidence table; not a spec constraint.

paper_verbatim: alternative compression baselines ... CFES, TD-MoE, M-SMoE
notes: Empirical table.

paper_verbatim: Mixtral-8x7B-Instruct-v0.1
notes: Mixtral-specific, not relevant to Qwen3 spec.

paper_verbatim: alternative ... on Mixtral
notes: Mixtral-specific.

paper_verbatim: 75% Parameter Retention on Qwen3-30B-A3B-Instruct-2507
notes: Closest paper regime to spec's overall retention; supports applicability.

paper_verbatim: CFES ... batch size=32 ... max length=4096
notes: Baseline-internal hyperparam, not used by spec.

paper_verbatim: MoBE ... 10000 epochs ... lr 0.07
notes: Baseline-internal.

paper_verbatim: HC-SMoE ... hierarchical/average linkage
notes: Baseline-internal.

paper_verbatim: TD-MoE ... ratio 0.4 ... SVD
notes: Baseline-internal.

paper_verbatim: M-SMoE ... router-logits
notes: Baseline-internal.

paper_verbatim: REAP ... 50/50 mixture of allenai/c4 and theblackcat102/evol-codealpaca-v1
notes: Baseline-stage calibration ≠ Router-KD calibration. Spec's REAP scoring is in Stage 2; calibration data audit is for that stage's scoring covariance, not Router KD.

spec_paraphrase: Spec implements Router KD as both Stage 2.5 (post-merge) and Stage 5 (post-EoRA).
paper_verbatim: We introduce Router Knowledge Distillation as a lightweight and general recovery strategy that updates only router parameters
notes: Spec adopts the contribution faithfully.

spec_paraphrase: Spec uses 'expert-frozen, router-trainable' as the operating mode for Router KD stages.
paper_verbatim: avoiding updates to expert parameters, while allowing lightweight router recalibration
notes: Spec inherits the paper's redefinition.

paper_verbatim: g̃^{orig,(A)}_i(x) ≠ g̃^{pruned,(A)}_i(x)
notes: Theory.

paper_verbatim: |y_orig − y^{best}_edit| ≤ Σ |g̃^{orig} E_i − g̃^{edit} X_i|
notes: Theory.

paper_verbatim: Pruning models exhibit a relatively larger divergence ... whereas Editing and Merging ... preserving the dimensionality of the router
notes: Spec uses merging+editing (lower router-drift paradigms), so router calibration is still warranted but less critical than for pruning. Supports spec's choice to run two router-KD stages.

paper_verbatim: as the layers deepen, the router increasingly selects experts different from those intended by the original model
notes: Empirical observation; spec doesn't reproduce.

spec_paraphrase: Stage 2.5 — Post-Merge Router Calibration: 'After Stage 2, the router has been resized but never retrained. ... Stage 5 corrects this. The full chain is: merge → heal routers (2.5) → factorize → compensate → heal routers again (5).'
paper_verbatim: effective MoE compression requires not only modifying experts, but also calibrating the router to remain consistent with the altered expert landscape
notes: Spec §5.5 directly implements the post-compression router-calibration mandate from §E.5/§5.5. Strong match.

paper_verbatim: EAC-MoE ... TopK-MSE objective
notes: Alternative objective for quantized MoE; not used (spec is not bit-level).

paper_verbatim: GEMQ ... lightweight global router fine-tuning
notes: Independent confirmation that router-only updates work; not directly used.

paper_verbatim: this difference will inevitably amplify in the Most Common and Worst scenarios
notes: Pruning-specific theory; spec uses merging + SVD.

paper_verbatim: the greater the imperfection in the Expert Editing method, the more the router's output diverges
notes: Editing-specific motivation for Stage 5 (post-SVD router calibration).

paper_verbatim: the beneficial impact of Router KD is diminished in coarse-grained MoE models like Mixtral
notes: Spec target (Qwen3) is in the strong-effect regime.

spec_paraphrase: Trains only mlp.gate.weight; all expert weights remain frozen.
paper_verbatim: updates only the student router parameters while keeping all other student parameters frozen
notes: Exact match.

spec_paraphrase: N_x = Σ_t m_{t+1} is the count of unmasked positions
paper_verbatim: N_x = Σ_{t=1}^{L−1} m_{t+1} + ε, where ε is a small constant
notes: Spec omits the +ε numerical-stability term in N_x. In practice (packed sequences, m=1 everywhere) ε is irrelevant — N_x=L−1>0 always — so this is a minor paraphrase drift, not a numerical deviation. Recommend: spec text could note 'with optional ε for numerical stability'.

spec_paraphrase: the gradient signal flows backward through the routing decisions, naturally adapting them
paper_verbatim: gradients flow through the (soft) gating weights of the selected experts during the forward pass, but parameter updates are restricted to the router
notes: Spec uses the standard HF MoE forward (soft top-k weighting on selected experts); router-only optimizer parameter group restricts updates.

paper_verbatim: Hybrid compression methods are intentionally excluded
notes: Spec IS hybrid (merge + SVD + EoRA + Router KD). The paper's experimental isolation does not bind the spec — Router KD is shown to work on each paradigm individually, and the spec applies it after each non-router transformation (Stage 2 merge → 2.5; Stage 3+4 SVD+EoRA → 5). This is a reasonable extension but is OUTSIDE the paper's evidence base. Surface as info.

spec_paraphrase: Effective batch size | 8 | Same as paper (8×1 = 2×4)
paper_verbatim: Batch size 2 ... Gradient accumulation steps 4
notes: Effective batch is identical (8). Spec uses bs=8/ga=1 instead of bs=2/ga=4 because H200 has VRAM headroom. Mathematically equivalent.

paper_verbatim: Calibration dataset c4 | Epochs 1 | Batch size 2 | ... Learning rate 5×10^−5 | Max sequence length 512 | KD temperature (T) 1.0 | Max calibration samples 3000
notes: Spec hyperparam table (line 528-543) does NOT specify LR scheduler or warmup. Paper Table 1 also omits these — implying constant LR. Spec is implicitly constant-LR (no scheduler row). Recommend: explicit 'LR schedule: constant (no warmup)' row in spec to remove ambiguity.

paper_verbatim: greedy decoding with a temperature of 0
notes: Eval-time decoding setting; relevant to Stage 6, not Stage 5/2.5.

paper_verbatim: exactly the same Router KD hyperparameters for every experiment case ... consistently used c4 as the calibration dataset
notes: Paper's generality claim assumes shared hyperparameters AND shared C4 calibration. Spec changes calibration data (D11) — interacts with this generality claim. The empirical equivalence of Nemotron-Cascade vs C4 for Router KD is unverified by the paper. This is a real risk surfaced by D11.

spec_paraphrase: Hardware: H200 ... combined VRAM ~126 GB
paper_verbatim: Qwen3 was tested in an environment equipped with NVIDIA A100 40GB GPUs
notes: Spec runs on H200 (141 GB) instead of A100-40GB. This is a hardware upgrade, not a numerical deviation — bf16 numerics are the same, larger VRAM enables single-device teacher+student loading. Should be confirmed in phase 6 (h200 crossref). Not in Ch.12 because hardware substitution is the spec's blanket H200 mandate (§8 Hardware line).

paper_verbatim: Mixtral was tested with A100 80GB GPUs
notes: Mixtral not in spec scope.

paper_verbatim: Router KD proves significantly more effective for the fine-grained Qwen3 (Left) compared to the coarse-grained Mixtral (Right)
notes: Empirical figure; supports spec's Qwen3 choice.

paper_verbatim: for the router outputs to be perfectly identical, this best-case scenario must be satisfied in every layer, which is statistically improbable
notes: Theory.

spec_paraphrase: Spec quotes this rationale directly to justify vocabulary-level (not router-gate) KD.
paper_verbatim: Router KD avoids requiring the teacher and student to share identical expert sets or gate dimensionalities
notes: Critical for spec because Stage 2 changes expert count (256→~180-200). Spec correctly uses vocabulary-level KD that survives this mismatch. Direct verbatim quote in spec.
